text: Very obvious that Banky is a peaceful, humble, level headed man. That is the secret ingredient in this relationship, one person must give more, there was no mention of Banky's love language, he has one too, yet he is okay to go along. In relationships the dominant partners get their way often times, this is the reality, you have it good when one of the partner is patient and understanding. God bless your marriage!
audit_reason: The comment discusses gendered relationship dynamics, mentioning the need for one partner to be more giving and patient, which implies a power/duty framing in relationships. It also touches on the concept of dominant partners, which is relevant to gender discourse.

text: @oluwafisayoosanyinbi4092 Yes thank you for mentioning, I probably didn't put it in a way that makes my thought clear, the message was about Banky, what he could give, and what she can receive, i believe his love language should also her about what he is getting back, it is a mutual benefit relationship. Anyways, not to cast aspersions, my comment only meant that relationships are always that way, one person giving and the other receiving. For it to work, one must patiently give for the other to keep receiving. It is not a criticism of their marriage, it is an endorsement that this is how relationships work and when people are in tune with that, they know peace.
audit_reason: The comment discusses relationship dynamics in terms of giving and receiving but lacks explicit gendered claims or masculinity discourse.
